title: teucrium polium subvar. pseudoaureum
intro: Teucrium polium subvar. pseudoaureum, commonly known as Golden Germander, is a subvariety of the Germander plant (Teucrium polium), a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Golden Germander include reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as its potential to help manage conditions such as arthritis and high blood pressure. Golden Germander can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies to support overall health and well-being.
